Q2
2020
Oceanside
Oceanside’s cash receipts from 
April through June were 6.9% above 
the second sales period in 2019. Ac­
tual sales were down 8.4% after ex­
cluding additional payments from 
last quarter that had been delayed 
by the State’s relief program to sup­
port businesses during the Covid-19 
crisis.
The March 19 State lockdown or­
ders temporarily shuttered many 
non-essential retailers and had a 
profound impact on most categories 
within the general consumer goods 
group including electronics, appli­
ance, and family apparel retailers.
Gasoline sales were also lower 
as many employees transitioned to 
working at home during the pan­
demic. The shuttering of restaurant
